overview.title: CCEM APM - Agentic Performance Monitor
overview.description: CCEM APM is a Phoenix/Elixir application that provides real-time monitoring, authorization, and orchestration for Claude Code agent sessions. It serves as the central nervous system for all agentic operations, tracking agent lifecycle events, tool calls, approvals, formations, and providing a comprehensive web dashboard at http://localhost:3032.
overview.methodology: OTP supervision tree architecture with ETS-backed caching, Phoenix PubSub for real-time broadcasts, and WebSocket channels for live dashboard updates.
overview.scope: Covers the APM server (Phoenix/Elixir), CCEMHelper (Swift macOS menu bar app), shell hooks, launchd services, and the complete API surface.

text.title: Architecture Overview
paragraphs: CCEM APM follows a microkernel + extensions architecture. The core provides fundamental monitoring primitives (agent lifecycle, sessions, notifications, health, telemetry), while extensions add domain-specific capabilities (AgentLock authorization, UPM project management, AG-UI protocol, Coalesce, Formations, etc.).

The OTP application starts under a one_for_one supervisor (ApmV5.Supervisor) with 50+ child processes organized into sub-supervisors: CoreSupervisor (config, dashboard, audit), AuthSupervisor (AgentLock), and AgUiSupervisorGroup (AG-UI protocol layer).

Data flows through ETS tables for high-performance reads, with Phoenix PubSub broadcasting state changes to connected LiveView clients. The Plugin system (PluginBehaviour) and Integration system (IntegrationBehaviour) provide standardized extension points.

CCEMHelper is a native Swift macOS menu bar application that connects to APM via HTTP polling and WebSocket, providing system-level notifications, approval UI, and quick actions without requiring a browser.

rows.purpose: HTTP/2 client for external API calls

text.title: Server Startup & Shutdown
paragraphs: The APM server runs as 'mix phx.server' from ~/Developer/ccem/apm-v4/. It binds to port 3032 and serves both the web dashboard (browser) and REST API (agents/hooks). The PID is stored at ~/Developer/ccem/apm-v4/.apm.pid.

On startup, the Application module initializes :inets and :ssl, attaches telemetry handlers, creates LifecycleMapper ETS tables, then starts the supervision tree with 50+ children in dependency order.
CRITICAL: CCEMHelper MUST be launched alongside the APM server. After starting mix phx.server, always run 'open -a CCEMHelper'.

callout.title: Always Start CCEMHelper with APM
callout.text: When running mix phx.server, ALWAYS also launch CCEMHelper immediately after. The companion app provides native notifications and approval UI that agents depend on.

text.title: CCEMHelper - Native macOS Companion
paragraphs: CCEMHelper is a Swift/AppKit macOS menu bar application at ~/Developer/ccem/CCEMHelper/. It monitors APM server health, displays agent notifications, handles approval requests, and provides quick actions.

Key services: APMClient (HTTP polling), APMEventStream (SSE), APMNotificationReceiver (push notifications), APMServerManager (lifecycle), UnixSocketTransport (native IPC), FormationMonitor, DriftDetector, UPMMonitor.
Views: MenuBarView (primary UI), ApprovalListView (AgentLock approvals), SettingsView, NotificationPermissionView, APMWebView (embedded dashboard).

rows.purpose: App entry point, menu bar setup
HTTP client for APM REST API
Native IPC via Unix domain socket

callout.title: Rebuilding CCEMHelper
callout.text: cd ~/Developer/ccem/CCEMHelper && swift build -c release && open -a CCEMHelper

text.title: launchd Service Labels
paragraphs: Both APM and CCEMHelper are managed via launchd with reverse-DNS labels under the io.pegues.agent-j.labs prefix.
APM Server label: io.pegues.agent-j.labs.ccem.apm-server
CCEMHelper label: io.pegues.agent-j.labs.ccem.helper

text.title: Health Check Endpoints
paragraphs: APM provides multiple health check endpoints with cached responses (1-2s TTL via StatusCache and DashboardData ETS tables).
GET /api/status -- Full system status including uptime, agent count, active sessions, and version.
GET /api/telemetry -- Telemetry metrics including memory usage, process count, and throughput.
The HealthCheckRunner GenServer performs periodic internal health checks on all critical subsystems.

kpis.label: StatusCache TTL
kpis.tooltip: ETS-backed cache for /api/status and /api/health hot paths

kpis.label: DashboardData TTL
kpis.tooltip: Preloaded mount data for LiveView dashboard

text.title: Supervision Hierarchy
paragraphs: ApmV5.Supervisor (one_for_one) is the top-level supervisor with 50+ direct and indirect children. Sub-supervisors group related processes:

CoreSupervisor: ConfigLoader, DashboardStore, AuditLog, DocsStore, ChatStore, ProjectStore, WorkflowRegistry, PlaneClient, AgentRegistry, AgentActivityLog, AgentIdentity, ConnectionTracker, EventStream, AlertRulesEngine, ExportManager, CommandRunner, BackfillRunner, UpmStore, UpmPersistentRule, PrdScanner, ApiKeyStore, PortManager, NotificationActionSchema, GraphBuilder.

AuthSupervisor: AuthorizationGate, PolicyEngine, SessionStore, TokenStore, ContextTracker, MemoryGate, RateLimiter, RedactionEngine, PendingDecisions, ApprovalQueue, ApprovalAuditLog, PolicyRulesStore, AutoApprovalStore, CommandContextExtractor.

AgUiSupervisorGroup: EventBus, EventBusHealth, EventRouter, StateManager, ToolCallTracker, SubscriptionStore, DashboardStateSync, HookBridge, MetricsBridge, AuditBridge, ActivityTracker, ApprovalGate, A2A Router/Addressing/Patterns.

text.title: Plugin Architecture (v8.0.0+)
paragraphs: Plugins implement PluginBehaviour with callbacks for: name, description, version, actions, nav_items, board, issues, and init. Each plugin is a GenServer managed by PluginSupervisor and discovered by PluginRegistry.
Integrations implement IntegrationBehaviour with callbacks for: name, description, actions, status, and init. They are managed by IntegrationSupervisor and discovered by IntegrationRegistry.

rows.name: SecurityGuidancePlugin
rows.description: v9.1.0 -- Bash injection, SSRF, prompt injection advisory detection (4 actions, dashboard widget)

rows.name: RalphPlugin
rows.description: Ralph autonomous fix loop tracking, flowchart visualization

rows.name: PlanePlugin
rows.description: Plane PM integration, issue sync, board view

rows.name: ClaudeCodePlugin
rows.description: Claude Code platform bridge, plugin discovery

rows.name: SkillsPlugin
rows.description: Skill registry, health scoring, dependency analysis

rows.name: UsagePlugin
rows.description: Claude model usage tracking per project

rows.name: AlertingPlugin
rows.description: Alert rules engine with threshold-based triggers

rows.name: MirofishPlugin
rows.description: Miro board integration for visual planning

rows.name: PortsPlugin
rows.description: Port management, conflict detection, namespace allocation

text.title: AgentLock Authorization System
paragraphs: AgentLock is the human-in-the-loop authorization gate for Claude Code tool calls. When an agent requests to execute a sensitive operation (Write, Bash, Edit, etc.), the request is intercepted by the pre_tool_use hook, sent to APM for authorization, and held until a human approves or rejects it.

The authorization flow: Agent hook -> POST /api/v2/auth/authorize -> PolicyEngine evaluates rules -> If auto-approve policy matches, grant immediately -> Otherwise queue in ApprovalQueue -> Notify via CCEMHelper native notification -> Human decides via ApprovalListView or dashboard -> Response sent back to hook.
Auto-approval policies support hierarchical scope matching with glob patterns, session-scoped or global lifetime, and can be created via the API or dashboard.
The ApprovalAuditLog maintains a complete history of all authorization decisions for compliance and debugging.

callout.title: AgentLock Auth Levels
callout.text: Auth levels: individual (single agent), squadron (formation group), global (all agents). Scope: read (view), write (modify), execute (run). Higher auth levels require explicit human approval.

text.title: AG-UI: Agent-to-UI Protocol
paragraphs: The AG-UI layer (based on ag_ui_ex ~> 0.1.0) provides a standardized protocol for agent-to-dashboard communication. Key components:
EventBus: Central pub/sub for AG-UI events (agent lifecycle, tool calls, state changes). EventBusHealth monitors throughput and latency.
EventRouter: Routes events to appropriate subscribers based on topic patterns. Topics module defines the topic hierarchy.
StateManager: Per-agent state storage with get/set/patch operations. Supports optimistic locking.
ToolCallTracker: Tracks tool call lifecycle (pending -> executing -> completed/failed). Provides real-time streaming via SSE.
DashboardStateSync: Synchronizes dashboard state across connected LiveView clients.
ApprovalGate: AG-UI companion that bridges AgentLock approvals into the event stream.
A2A (Agent-to-Agent): Messaging subsystem with addressing, envelope format, router, and fan-out/broadcast patterns.

text.title: Core REST API (v1)
paragraphs: Base path: /api. All endpoints accept/return JSON. CORS enabled. API auth via ApiAuth plug.
Full OpenAPI spec available at GET /api/v2/openapi.json (canonical) or GET /api/openapi.json (alias).

text.title: Extended REST API (v2)
paragraphs: Base path: /api/v2. Provides enhanced agent management, SLOs, alerts, workflows, formations, chat, A2A messaging, and approvals.

rows.endpoints: authorize, execute, summary, tools, sessions, tokens, policy/rules, auto-approval-policies, pending, decide, api-keys
projects CRUD, pm_integrations, vcs_integrations, work_items, sync, drift report
emit, tool, events SSE, state get/set/patch, router/stats, diagnostics
index, reload, show, action, board, issues, repositories CRUD, cc bridge
start, preview, show, diff, gate/decide, apply, cancel
index, summary, project, record, limits, reset
agents, skills, commands, mcp, tools, hooks, patterns, learnings, graph

text.title: Phoenix Channels
paragraphs: WebSocket endpoint: UserSocket. Channels provide real-time bidirectional communication for dashboard features.

text.title: Standard Startup Procedure
paragraphs: Follow this exact sequence to start the CCEM APM system.

items: Check for existing APM processes: lsof -ti:3032
Kill stale processes if found: lsof -ti:3032 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null
Start APM server: cd ~/Developer/ccem/apm-v4 && nohup mix phx.server > /Volumes/DDRV902/logs/apm_server.log 2>&1 & echo $! > .apm.pid

callout.title: Formation Agent Registration
callout.text: Every formation agent MUST register with APM on spawn using fire-and-forget curl to /api/register with full upm_context metadata. No exceptions.

accordion.title: APM server won't start / port 3032 in use
accordion.content: Run: lsof -ti:3032 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null && pkill -9 -f 'mix phx.server'. Then restart normally.

accordion.title: CCEMHelper not showing notifications
accordion.content: 1. Check notification permissions in System Preferences > Notifications. 2. Verify APM is running: curl -s http://localhost:3032/api/status. 3. Rebuild: cd ~/Developer/ccem/CCEMHelper && swift build -c release && open -a CCEMHelper.

accordion.title: Dashboard shows stale data
accordion.content: StatusCache and DashboardData have 1-2s TTL. If data is truly stale: POST /api/config/reload to force refresh. Check PubSub subscriptions in browser console.

accordion.title: Agent registration fails
accordion.content: Verify APM is running and accepting requests. Check the agent_id format (must be string). Ensure Content-Type: application/json header is set. Review /Volumes/DDRV902/logs/apm_server.log for errors.

accordion.title: mix compile fails with warnings-as-errors
accordion.content: Run mix compile without the flag first to see all warnings. Common issues: unused variables (prefix with _), missing @spec annotations, deprecated function calls.

accordion.title: Lottie animation stall
accordion.content: This was a known issue fixed in a recent patch. If animations stall in the dashboard, hard-refresh the page (Cmd+Shift+R). If persistent, check the JS console for errors in the Lottie player.

accordion.title: Dep graph race condition
accordion.content: The D3.js dependency graph had a race condition with data loading. Fixed by deferring graph render until data load completes. If graph is blank, wait 2 seconds then click Refresh in the graph toolbar.

text.title: CCEMHelper Stall Permanent Fix
paragraphs: A permanent fix for CCEMHelper stalls was recently applied. The root cause was the event stream SSE connection holding the main thread when the APM server became unresponsive.
The fix moves all network operations to background actors and implements a watchdog timer that detects stalls and automatically reconnects.

callout.title: Force Kill When Stalled
callout.text: If the APM server is completely unresponsive: lsof -ti:3032 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null && pkill -9 -f 'mix phx.server'. Then restart from the startup runbook.

items: Restart: cd ~/Developer/ccem/apm-v4 && nohup mix phx.server > /Volumes/DDRV902/logs/apm_server.log 2>&1 & echo $! > .apm.pid

text.title: Disk Space Awareness
paragraphs: The system disk frequently approaches capacity. Log files in /private/tmp/ and /Volumes/DDRV902/logs/ can grow large. ETS tables are in-memory but Erlang crash dumps (erl_crash.dump) can be significant.
Regular cleanup targets: /private/tmp/*.log, ~/Developer/ccem/apm-v4/erl_crash.dump, Xcode DerivedData, Docker images.

callout.title: Disk Space Monitoring
callout.text: Run 'df -h /' regularly. When available space drops below 500MB, Claude Code Bash commands will fail with ENOSPC. Truncate log files and clear caches immediately.

items: Clear APM build: cd ~/Developer/ccem/apm-v4 && mix clean
Check large files: du -sh /private/tmp/* | sort -rh | head -20

text.title: Security Native Types Feature
paragraphs: v9.1.0 introduced security as a first-class plugin_scope atom. The SecurityGuidancePlugin (ApmV5.Plugins.Security.SecurityGuidancePlugin) provides 4 security-aware actions with a dashboard widget.

Wave 1 (Python hooks): Expanded security_reminder_hook.py with Bash command injection detection, WebFetch/WebSearch SSRF detection (IMDS/schemes blocking, advisory on private ranges), and Agent/Skill prompt injection advisory detection. hooks.json matcher updated to cover Edit|Write|MultiEdit|Bash|WebFetch|WebSearch|Agent|Skill.
Wave 2 (Elixir backend): Added :security as first-class plugin_scope to PluginBehaviour. Created SecurityGuidancePlugin with 4 actions and a dashboard widget.
Wave 3 (Tests): 41-test suite covering contract compliance, all 4 actions, endpoints, navigation items, and widget rendering.

text.title: Human-in-the-Loop Approval Flow
paragraphs: The approval workflow ensures sensitive operations require human authorization before execution.

timeline.title: Hook Intercept
timeline.description: pre_tool_use hook intercepts tool call and sends to APM

timeline.title: Policy Evaluation
timeline.description: PolicyEngine checks auto-approval policies and rules

timeline.title: Queue / Auto-Approve
timeline.description: If no auto-approve match, request queued in ApprovalQueue with debouncing

timeline.title: Notification
timeline.description: CCEMHelper receives notification via APMNotificationReceiver, shows native alert

timeline.title: Human Decision
timeline.description: User approves/rejects via ApprovalListView, dashboard, or API

timeline.title: Audit Log
timeline.description: Decision recorded in ApprovalAuditLog for compliance

timeline.title: Response
timeline.description: Authorization result returned to waiting hook

text.title: PolicyEngine & Auto-Approval
paragraphs: The PolicyEngine (ApmV5.Auth.PolicyEngine) evaluates authorization requests against configured rules. PolicyRulesStore maintains tool-level rules. AutoApprovalStore manages hierarchical auto-approval policies.
Auto-approval policies support: glob pattern matching on tool names and parameters, session-scoped or global lifetime, priority ordering, and test-match endpoint for validation.
Rate limiting (ApmV5.Auth.RateLimiter) prevents abuse. Redaction (ApmV5.Auth.RedactionEngine) sanitizes sensitive data in audit logs.

rows.purpose: Test if a request matches any policy

text.title: Installation
paragraphs: CCEM APM requires Elixir 1.15+/OTP 26+ and macOS 14+ for CCEMHelper.

items: Clone: git clone https://github.com/peguesj/ccem.git && cd ccem/apm-v4
Or manual: mix deps.get && mix setup
Build CCEMHelper: cd ~/Developer/ccem/CCEMHelper && swift build -c release

callout.title: install.sh Options
callout.text: --prefix <path> (custom dir), --skip-service (no launchd), --skip-hooks (no settings.json patch), --skip-agent (no CCEMHelper build), --dry-run, --yes (non-interactive)

rows.purpose: Claude Code hook configuration (pre/post tool use)

text.title: Version History & Upgrade Notes
paragraphs: The APM follows semantic versioning. Major features by version:

timeline.title: v9.1.0 - Security Native Types
timeline.description: SecurityGuidancePlugin with :security scope, Bash injection/SSRF/prompt injection detection, 41-test suite

timeline.title: v9.0.0 - Architecture Store
timeline.description: Diligent fleet hierarchy, Proxy layer (Cache + RateLimiter), HookRegistry, Widget system

timeline.title: v8.12.0 - Concurrency Layer
timeline.description: ConcurrencyLayer, JobQueue, BootReporter, WorktreeStore, JSON formatter

timeline.title: v6.4.0 - Skills UX
timeline.description: Skills dashboard overhaul, Claude Usage Tracking, Port Management

callout.title: Upgrade Procedure
callout.text: 1. Stop APM (kill port 3032). 2. git pull. 3. mix deps.get && mix compile --warnings-as-errors. 4. Rebuild CCEMHelper if Swift changes. 5. Restart both services.
